findings.evidence: Grep shows arbitrary coefficient lemmas at lines 199 and 206, while the only exported support lemma is the integral special case at line 269: `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff`.
findings.fix: Add a characteristic lemma such as `@[simp] lemma mem_support_principalDivisor_iff ... : v ∈ ...support ↔ v.valuation K ((Additive.toMul u : Kˣ) : K) ≠ 1`.
findings.issue: The public API characterizes coefficients of arbitrary Dedekind principal divisors but not support membership, so users cannot rewrite `v ∈ ((OrderSystem.ofDedekindDomain R K).principalDivisor u).support` to the valuation condition without unfolding through `Finsupp.mem_support_iff` and `WithZero.log`.

findings.evidence: Line 269 declares `lemma mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff ...` with no attribute, unlike nearby normal-form lemmas such as `adicOrd_apply` at line 77 and `OrderSystem.ofDedekindDomain_ord` at line 193.
findings.fix: Mark `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff` with `@[simp]`, unless superseded by a more general support simp lemma that specializes to the same normal form.
findings.issue: `mem_support_principalDivisor_of_integral_iff` is the advertised `mem_*_iff` normal form for divisors of integral elements, but it has no `@[simp]` attribute, so `simp` will not reduce this support membership to `r ∈ v.asIdeal`.

summary: The Dedekind order-system surface still lacks a support characterization for arbitrary principal divisors, and the integral support normal form is not exposed to automation.
